rubric.rubric_intent: Evaluate the soundness, backend-agnosticism, and empirical falsifiability of a debias-scaffold (HERMES-BLIND) design plan.

dimensions.name: Backend Abstraction
dimensions.description: Measures the degree to which the design explicitly isolates components from backend-specific implementation details.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Examine the design document for descriptions of modularity and interfaces; identify sections outlining backend dependencies and how they are abstracted; check for the use of dependency injection or similar patterns.

dimensions.name: Falsifiability Strategy
dimensions.description: Assesses the clarity and feasibility of the proposed strategies for empirically testing and potentially disproving the design’s claims.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Locate the section detailing evaluation methodologies; identify specific, testable hypotheses related to debiasing; analyze the proposed metrics and experimental setup for potential biases or limitations.

dimensions.name: Debiasing Technique Specificity
dimensions.description: Evaluates how specifically the debiasing techniques are described, including data preprocessing, model adjustments, and output filtering.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Search for detailed explanations of debiasing strategies, including specific algorithms, parameter choices, and rationales; identify potential failure modes and mitigation plans.

dimensions.name: Error Handling & Edge Cases
dimensions.description: Measures the design’s consideration of potential error conditions and edge cases, especially those related to biased data or unexpected inputs.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Look for sections describing error handling procedures; identify anticipated edge cases (e.g., zero-shot prompts, adversarial examples); assess the design’s response to potential failures or unexpected results.

dimensions.name: Modularity & Testability
dimensions.description: Assesses the degree to which the design is broken down into independent, testable modules.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Examine the design document for descriptions of modular components and their interactions; identify clear interfaces between modules; look for descriptions of unit testing and integration testing strategies.

dimensions.name: Reproducibility Reporting
dimensions.description: Evaluates the plan's provision for detailed, auditable reproducibility receipts documenting the entire design, training, and evaluation process.
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Check for dedicated sections outlining the reproducibility report format, including specifics on data hashes, prompt versions, and backend environments.

dimensions.name: Scalability Considerations
dimensions.description: Measures how well the design addresses potential scalability challenges arising from increased data volume, complexity, or user load (hedge: true).
dimensions.evidence_instructions: Look for mentions of scalability, including how data preprocessing, model training, and inference will be handled at scale; assess the design's anticipated resource requirements and potential bottlenecks.

citations.quote: prepend to every backend call in `backends.py`
citations.location: Integration points

citations.quote: add as a fourth rule in the polished system prompt
citations.location: Integration points

citations.quote: the scaffold's effect should be visible in the disclosed "prior exposure" line so reviewers can inspect what the model "knew"
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)

citations.quote: prepend as a string, no API changes needed
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The document outlines specific integration points for the 'hermes-blind' scaffold, detailing how it should be applied to different backends, including within `backends.py` and `rolitwin` integration. It specifies that the implementation requires no API changes and that the scaffold’s impact should be readily visible in the model's output.

citations.quote: A short (~40-token) language scaffold, prepended to any scoring prompt, can reduce all four biases by forcing: Disclosure of prior exposure...
citations.location: Hypothesis

citations.quote: Score using only quoted evidence from the target text below.
citations.location: Draft scaffold — HERMES-BLIND v1

citations.quote: variance reduction ≥30% AND cross-model std ≤1.0 AND signal correlation preserved within ±0.1
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)

citations.quote:
1. Use the 4-paper `applied/` corpus in hermes-rubric as target set
2. Score each paper 5x with BLIND + 5x without, on each of {Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, qwen3.5:9b via Ollama}
citations.location: Test protocol (Phase 4 in the meta-plan)

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The design proposes a scaffold to reduce bias by mandating disclosure, evidence-gating, hedging, and output shape discipline.  The plan outlines specific, measurable criteria for success (variance reduction, convergence, signal preservation) and includes a concrete test protocol using a defined corpus and multiple LLMs to validate the effectiveness of the scaffold.

citations.quote: Score using only quoted evidence from the target text below.
citations.location: ## Draft scaffold — HERMES-BLIND v1

citations.quote: hedging on thin evidence
citations.location: evidence selection stage itself can be biased

citations.quote: evidence-gate makes prior-derived claims inadmissible independent of disclosure.
citations.location: ## Known failure modes to design against

citations.quote: hedge only on thin evidence, not by default.
citations.location: ## Known failure modes to design against

citations.quote: scaffold must be intent-agnostic (no domain-specific language; works identically for any target).
citations.location: ## Known failure modes to design against

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The design explicitly details several debiasing techniques, including evidence-gating (only using quoted text), hedging based on thin evidence, and ensuring the scaffold is intent-agnostic to prevent signal contamination.  The document also outlines mitigation strategies to ensure these techniques function as intended.

citations.quote: force a structural output ("state in one line") so non-compliance is visible.
citations.location: Known failure modes to design against

citations.quote: evidence-gate makes prior-derived claims inadmissible independent of disclosure.
citations.location: Known failure modes to design against

citations.quote: hedge only on thin evidence, not by default.
citations.location: Known failure modes to design against

citations.quote: scaffold must be intent-agnostic (no domain-specific language; works identically for any target).
citations.location: Known failure modes to design against

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The design explicitly anticipates several failure modes related to bias and unexpected model behavior, outlining mitigation strategies that involve structural output constraints, evidence gating, controlled hedging, and scaffold intent-agnosticism to ensure robust error handling and edge case management.

citations.quote: prepend to every backend call in `backends.py`
citations.location: Integration points

citations.quote: add as a fourth rule in the polished system prompt
citations.location: Integration points

citations.quote: reduce score variance on repeated runs of the same target by ≥30%
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)

citations.quote: Improve cross-model score convergence (std across backends ≤1.0 on a 0-10 scale for the same target)
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)

citations.quote: the scaffold's effect should be visible in the disclosed "prior exposure" line
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The design explicitly details integration points for the `hermes-blind` scaffold within existing infrastructure (`backends.py`, `rolitwin`) and outlines concrete success criteria including variance reduction, convergence, and auditability via disclosure of 'prior exposure'.

citations.quote: the scaffold’s effect should be visible in the disclosed "prior exposure" line so reviewers can inspect what the model "knew"
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)
Test protocol (Phase 4 in the meta-plan)

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The plan explicitly states the need for visibility into the model's 'prior exposure' through the scaffold and outlines a test protocol that includes scoring with and without the scaffold across multiple models to assess its effect.

citations.quote: Be ~40 tokens or less (negligible per-call cost; wraps at scale)
citations.location: Success criteria (what makes this plan good)

citations.quote: Total: 4 papers × 2 conditions × 5 runs × 4 models = 160 scoring runs.
citations.location: Test protocol (Phase 4 in the meta-plan)

evidence_citations.evidence_summary: The design explicitly mentions that the scaffold's token count is a key consideration for scalability, aiming for a 'negligible per-call cost' by keeping it under 40 tokens.  The planned test protocol also outlines a significant number (160) of scoring runs which implies considerations for managing the computational load associated with large-scale testing.

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The design mentions specific integration points within backend files (`backends.py`, `rolitwin`), suggesting the abstraction isn't fully complete but is rather a layered approach with explicit backend integration. However, the requirement of no API changes and visible impact in model output indicates an attempt to minimize backend dependencies and observable alterations.
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: prepend to every backend call in `backends.py` [Integration points]

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The design proposes specific, measurable criteria for success (variance reduction, convergence, signal preservation) and a concrete test protocol involving a defined corpus and multiple LLMs. This demonstrates a clear and feasible approach to empirically testing and potentially disproving the design's claims. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: “variance reduction ≥30% AND cross-model std ≤1.0 AND signal correlation preserved within ±0.1” [Success criteria (what makes this plan good)]

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The document provides explicit details on several debiasing techniques including evidence-gating, hedging, and ensuring intent-agnosticism, with outlined mitigation strategies. The level of detail provided suggests a well-considered and specific approach. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]

The document also outlines mitigation strategies to ensure these techniques function as intended.

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The design proactively addresses multiple failure modes related to bias and unexpected model behavior using specific techniques like structural output constraints, evidence gating, and intent-agnostic scaffolding. This demonstrates a strong focus on robust error handling and edge case management. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]
per_dim_scores.evidence_drove_score: The design explicitly anticipates several failure modes related to bias and unexpected model behavior, outlining mitigation strategies that involve structural output constraints, evidence gating, controlled hedging, and scaffold intent-agnosticism to ensure robust error handling and edge case management.

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The design demonstrates modularity by explicitly detailing integration points within existing infrastructure (`backends.py`, `rolitwin`) and providing specific, measurable success criteria. While integration points are clear, the level of independent testability isn't explicitly discussed, preventing a higher score. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The plan outlines a test protocol involving scoring with and without a scaffold across multiple models, and aims for visibility into prior exposure. This demonstrates a commitment to allowing review of the model's knowledge, contributing to reproducibility, though detail on specific documentation beyond this isn't evident.

per_dim_scores.score_rationale: The design explicitly addresses scalability by targeting a low token count per call and the test protocol plans for a substantial number of runs, suggesting attention to computational load. While the design doesn't detail a comprehensive scalability strategy, these aspects demonstrate initial considerations. [Score capped at 6: all citations are README/doc (self-marketing); no code/test evidence.]

inputs.intent: evaluate whether this design plan for a debias-scaffold (HERMES-BLIND) is sound, backend-agnostic, and empirically falsifiable

receipt.reproducibility_note: Same inputs + same backend + same model version + same rubric_hash should produce scores within ±1 point. A rubric_hash diff between runs means the measuring stick itself changed — scores are not directly comparable.
